_comment: Model pack v2 manifest. See models/MODEL_PACK_V2.md for the schema reference. Paths are resolved relative to this file. sha256 is verified at engine load time when artifact verification is wired up (W-DETECT D5).
_generated_by: models/tools/generate_model_pack.py (or hand-edited at the repo root for the ship-in-tree models below)

models._comment: YOLOv26-nano. As of v0.1.19 the engine ships three STATIC-shape ONNX exports — yolo26n_640.onnx, yolo26n_960.onnx, yolo26n_1280.onnx — one per supported input size. Static shapes are mandatory for the Intel NPU plugin (which silently falls back to CPU on dynamic-shape models, observed on Lunar Lake k13) and let the OpenVINO blob cache hit on subsequent boots. Per-tier defaults: T10/T24=640, T36/T36-S=960, T64=640 (override per-camera to 1280 for plate/face detail). The legacy `yolo26n_dynamic.onnx` is deprecated and no longer shipped — regenerate with `python tools/models/gen_yolo26n.py --all-static` before tagging a release.

presets._comment: Default operating point. Best precision/latency tradeoff on T10 / T24 (Iris Xe iGPU). Engine resolves this preset to yolo26n_640.onnx.
Mid operating point. Default on T36 (Arc A380 dGPU) and T36-S (Lunar Lake NPU 4). Engine resolves this preset to yolo26n_960.onnx.

Highest-precision operating point. Opt-in per-camera override for plate / face / small-object work. Quadruples compute vs. 640; only enable on tiers with NPU/dGPU headroom. Engine resolves this preset to yolo26n_1280.onnx.

models._comment: YOLO-World v2 (small) export. As of v0.1.22 the engine ships per-tier STATIC-shape ONNXs — yolo_world_v2_s_640.onnx (T10/T24 default) and yolo_world_v2_s_960.onnx (T36/T36-S default). Static shapes are mandatory for the Intel NPU plugin (silent CPU fallback on dynamic shapes, observed on Lunar Lake k13 under v0.1.18–0.1.20) and let the OpenVINO blob cache hit on subsequent boots. Prompts are baked into the graph at export time; per-camera config picks a subset at runtime. Regenerate via `python tools/models/gen_yolo_world.py --all-static` whenever the prompt vocabulary changes — the manifest sha256 values below will refresh and the engine's loader will catch the diff. 1280 is intentionally NOT shipped (overkill for the coarse semantic queries open-vocab is used for); the YOLOE multi-size export is deferred to M3.4 (prompt-set rework). NOTE on task strings: as of M3.1 the manifest recognises three open-vocab task tags: 'detect_open_vocab_text' (yolo_world + yoloe text mode), 'detect_open_vocab_visual' (yoloe visual mode), and 'detect_open_vocab' kept as a backward-compat alias for 'detect_open_vocab_text'. The Rust loader keys off the engine's `inference.model.kind` config, not this task field.

YOLOE (small) text-mode export. Prompts are baked into the graph at export time; per-camera config picks a subset at runtime. Regenerate via tools/models/gen_yoloe.py whenever the prompt vocabulary changes — the manifest sha256 below will refresh and the engine's loader will catch the diff.
